title: Remote Management: Motivating a Disengaged Virtual Team
summary: Your remote team's energy is dropping—cameras off, short replies, missed deadlines. Re-engage without micromanaging: connection before correction.

scene.setting: Your team has been remote for months. Cameras are off, replies are one-word, and two deadlines were missed last week. You suspect disengagement but aren't sure if it's burnout, isolation, or something else. A virtual team call is starting in five minutes.

beats.narration: The call starts. Two cameras are off. One person joins late. The energy is flat. The leadership challenge is re-engaging without surveillance or guilt.

dialogue.speaker: lead
dialogue.line: Before we dive in—how is everyone doing? Not the project, you personally.

choices.label: Require cameras on and set stricter daily check-ins.

steps.step: Before adding rules, schedule one 15-min 1:1 with each person to ask: 'What's working? What's not?'

leader_lines: Control feels productive but erodes trust.
What would I want from my leader right now?
questions: What's one thing that would make your day easier?
scripts.closing_line: One 1:1 per person this week; listen before legislating.

choices.label: Start the call with a genuine check-in: 'How are you doing—personally?'

steps.step: Open with a personal check-in; share your own state first to model vulnerability.
Ask each person one thing: 'What's one thing that would help you this week?'

leader_lines: I'll go first—here's how I'm doing.
questions: What's one thing that would help you this week?
What's draining your energy right now?
scripts.closing_line: Model vulnerability; ask what helps; follow through on one thing.

choices.label: Schedule individual 1:1s to understand each person's situation.

steps.step: Schedule 15-min 1:1 with each team member within 48 hours.
In each 1:1: 'What's working? What's not? What would help?'
Compile themes and share back with the team (anonymized); pick one change to try for two weeks.

leader_lines: I want to understand before I fix anything.
Your experience matters more than my assumptions.
questions: Do you feel connected to the team?
scripts.closing_line: 1:1s this week; compile themes; one change for two weeks.

choices.label: Send an email reminding the team about expectations and deadlines.

steps.step: Before sending the email, call one person and ask how they're doing. Start with connection.

leader_lines: Emails about expectations don't fix disengagement.
What if the problem isn't laziness but isolation?
questions: When did I last have a real conversation with a team member?
What am I avoiding by sending an email instead of calling?
scripts.closing_line: Call one person first; connection before correction.

system_prompts.nudge: Disengagement is often a signal, not a choice. Listen before you legislate.

outcomes.message: The team complied but engagement dropped further. One person is quietly job-searching.
reflection_questions: What would have changed if you'd listened first?
How do you build trust you can't see?

outcomes.message: The team felt heard. Energy improved. One person shared a personal struggle they'd been hiding. Trust grew.
reflection_questions: How do you sustain connection over distance?

tsp.weekly_pulse_prompt: This week I connected with a remote team member beyond task updates. (1-5)
